title: manihot tristis subsp. surumuensis
intro: Manihot tristis subsp. surumuensis, also known as Surumu cassava or Manihot surumuensis, is a subspecies of the Manihot tristis plant, a type of shrub or small tree native to the Amazon rainforest. The plant is known for its edible roots, which contain various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Manihot tristis subsp. surumuensis include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, such as fever and gastrointestinal issues. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often used to make a tea or infusion, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
